confidence_rationale: Direct single-unit OFC recording in macaque during economic choice. Shows OFC encodes good identities and values in juice-based 'labeled lines' -- distinct neural populations encode distinct juice identities. This is THE most architecturally direct evidence for option A's labeled-line / one-hot identity coding at the OFC level. Confidence high on source quality (Padoa-Schioppa lab, peer-reviewed Curr Biol). Mapping fidelity strong: 'good identity' in this paper maps cleanly onto SD-049's resource-type identity. Transfer risk moderate -- macaque OFC vs REE substrate, but the architectural primitive (per-juice-identity neural populations) is the cleanest precedent.
failure_signatures: If option A wins (one-hot slot identity coding) and ARM_2 still fails to produce goal_resource_r >= 0.5 at V3-EXQ-514, the labeled-line architecture is not the bottleneck -- check identity-classifier head training (P0 phase) and the supervision signal quality, not the architectural choice.

If labeled-line representation produces brittle generalisation across distinct grid configurations of the same type, REE may need to add the similarity-preserving layer that Schapiro 2016 substrate requires (i.e. fall back to option C hybrid).

mapping.source_claim_statement: OFC neurons recorded during sequential-offer juice choice encoded good identities and values in a juice-based representation (labeled lines). Distinct neural populations encoded distinct juice identities; values within each identity were encoded by neurons of that population. Decisions appear to involve circuit inhibition, where each offer value indirectly inhibits neurons encoding the opposite choice outcome.
mapping.ree_translation: OFC's biological architecture for encoding distinct outcome identities is labeled-line: one population per identity, with within-population coding of value/magnitude on top. This is the precise architectural signature SD-049 option A proposes: one-hot identity slot (the labeled line) concatenated with magnitude latent (the within-line value coding). The Ballesta-Padoa-Schioppa finding is the cleanest direct biological precedent for option A in the cohort of papers reviewed here.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Mapping fidelity is high but not perfect. (1) OFC labeled-line populations may emerge from a distributed substrate upstream (Schapiro 2016 / Howard 2015 cross-tag) rather than being substrate-level labeled lines. (2) The juice-based representation in this paper is task-locked: the animal has been trained on these specific juice options. SD-049's defaults (food / water / novelty) are categorically distinct in a way the trained-juice paradigm is not. (3) The decision mechanism (circuit inhibition) is not what z_resource implements -- z_resource is a pre-decision identity representation, not the choice-resolution mechanism. The architectural primitive that transfers is the separate-population-per-identity claim, not the inhibition mechanism.
mapping.source_context: Single-unit recording in OFC (areas 11/13) of two macaques choosing between two juices presented sequentially in a delayed match-to-sample paradigm. Compared firing patterns across simultaneous-offer and sequential-offer conditions; the labeled-line finding holds across both modalities.

confidence_components.notes: Padoa-Schioppa is a leading OFC electrophysiologist; the labeled-line vs distributed-coding question has been a focus of his lab for over a decade. High mapping fidelity because 'good identity' is precisely the variable SD-049 wants z_resource to carry. Transfer risk is moderate -- macaque OFC vs REE substrate -- but the architectural primitive transfers cleanly enough that this is the strongest single biological signal for option A in the slate.
